{

  "riskReason": "Timing-safe comparison and hardcoded secret already flagged by L1; additional issues with encryption key validation and webhook header case-sensitivity create security gaps.",

      "severity": "high",
      "category": "security",
      "file": "packages/app-store/_utils/oauth/parseRefreshTokenResponse.ts",
      "line": 27,
      "title": "Hardcoded fallback refresh_token value",
      "description": "Line 27 sets `refreshTokenResponse.data.refresh_token = \"refresh_token\"` as a hardcoded string literal when no refresh token is present in the OAuth response. This creates a non-functional credential that will fail silently on token refresh attempts, potentially locking users out of integrations without clear error messaging.",
      "suggestion": "Remove the hardcoded fallback or throw an error explicitly when refresh_token is missing from the OAuth provider response, forcing proper error handling upstream."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "security",
      "file": "apps/web/pages/api/webhook/app-credential.ts",
      "line": 26,
      "title": "Case-sensitive webhook header lookup with environment variable",
      "description": "The webhook secret header comparison uses `process.env.CALCOM_WEBHOOK_HEADER_NAME || \"calcom-webhook-secret\"` directly in `req.headers[]` lookup. HTTP headers are case-insensitive per RFC 7230, but Node.js normalizes incoming headers to lowercase. If CALCOM_WEBHOOK_HEADER_NAME contains uppercase letters, the header lookup will always fail, bypassing webhook authentication.",
      "suggestion": "Normalize the header name to lowercase: `req.headers[((process.env.CALCOM_WEBHOOK_HEADER_NAME || \"calcom-webhook-secret\").toLowerCase())]`"
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "security",
      "file": "apps/web/pages/api/webhook/app-credential.ts",
      "line": 59,
      "title": "Missing encryption key validation before decryption",
      "description": "The `symmetricDecrypt` call on line 59 uses `process.env.CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY || \"\"` with an empty string fallback. If the environment variable is missing, decryption will fail silently or produce garbage data. The .env.example specifies the key must be 32 bytes for AES256, but no validation or length check occurs before use.",
      "suggestion": "Validate that CALCOM_APP_CREDENTIAL_ENCRYPTION_KEY exists and is exactly 32 bytes before attempting decryption. Return a 500 error if missing or invalid."
    }
  ]
}
